title: A look at the rebuilt characters in prediction of 40
display_title: A look at the rebuilt characters in prediction of 40
wikitext:
[]: A look at the rebuilt characters in prediction of 4.0             Quote Post by Zeph » Thu Mar 05, 2015 11:19 pm Lately I've taken to really getting into movies, books, and stories to really understand them and see what makes them tick. My goal through doing this is to really help my storytelling capability for future endeavors. I imagined that I would do a big writeup on Rebuild after 4.44 got delivered to me, but a few weeks ago I saw Rebuild in a new light and started writing this up. It's more or less a first draft, but any rewrite would still carry the character/writing ideas I've looked at. Part One: Seeing Rebuild as a Rebuild SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 05, 2015 11:22 pm That's a lot of uses of "Rebuild/Rebuilt" for something that isn't actually ca-- I'll try to read it in the morning.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 3:00 am Coo! Lots to think about, and on a first run through, lots that has parallels with some of my thoughts on how things progress. I need to mull it over at length, though.

[]: Re: A look at the rebuilt characters in prediction of 4.0             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 06, 2015 4:13 am Zeph wrote: Characters in 2.22 were named the same and performed roughly the same actions, but their characteristics were almost completely opposite what they appeared to be from the original series. ... Out of nostalgia I tried going back and watching the original episodes to get a better idea of what the writers could have pulled from the characters. It was hard watching the old episodes and honestly a bit confusing. The characters are soo different I had a hard time remembering things about them because of their different Rebuild selves.

[]: Re: A look at the rebuilt characters in prediction of 4.0             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 4:48 am Mr. Tines wrote: ... reaffirms the cognitive dissonance ...

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Mar 06, 2015 7:11 am pwhodges wrote: Wouldn't you first need a view of your own to have it reaffirmed by someone else? At the moment it seems that you're just rationalising your lack of a first-hand view.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Mar 06, 2015 9:02 am I love how Tines never gives up to voice his opinion about the Rebuilds, without ever having seen them. That's true perseverance! (though I guess, at this point his so biased against rebuild, even if he watched it, he wouldn't be able to approach it without hating it outright. A vicious circle)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Mar 06, 2015 10:13 am robersora wrote: I love how Tines never gives up to voice his opinion about the Rebuilds, without ever having seen them. That's true perseverance!

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 10:19 am The point which I immediately felt unhappy with is the deduction that Touji has an abusive relationship with Asuka. This seems to me a step too far, especially given Asuka's other strengths (though the damage done by Q-Bardiel may affect them). But the idea of a relationship between them sits moderately well with me (though it leaves Hikari out in the cold - Kensuke x Hikari, anyone?).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 06, 2015 10:56 am For my devoted fans, I submit into evidence the yards of bile about Rebuild vs original characterization that I had to moderate (while most everyone else was staying clear of spoilers) in threads like this one -- http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11561/ For a film to generate quite so much hate on that score amongst those who did watch it is a strong signal that there might be more than a smidgen of a difference between the portrayals. Zeph's statement on how going back to the original from the later take was jarring merely affirms that.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeph » Fri Mar 06, 2015 11:44 am pwhodges wrote: The point which I immediately felt unhappy with is the deduction that Touji has an abusive relationship with Asuka. This seems to me a step too far, especially given Asuka's other strengths (though the damage done by Q-Bardiel may affect them). But the idea of a relationship between them sits moderately well with me (though it leaves Hikari out in the cold - Kensuke x Hikari, anyone?).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:11 pm pwhodges wrote: The point which I immediately felt unhappy with is the deduction that Touji has an abusive relationship with Asuka. This seems to me a step too far, especially given Asuka's other strengths (though the damage done by Q-Bardiel may affect them). But the idea of a relationship between them sits moderately well with me (though it leaves Hikari out in the cold - Kensuke x Hikari, anyone?).

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:19 pm There is also the possibility of a connection between Asuka and Touji mirroring the deleted live-action segment from EoE , I suppose - at any rate, it came to mind. But a specific reason for Asuka probably not being a mother is that Nerv or Wille (in turn) could not have tolerated her being unavailable for piloting during times of crisis for such a trivial (from the perspective of the times) reason.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:33 pm ^ That, and for whatever reason she’s not grown any taller in the past 14 years, nor has she seemed to have had any other physical changes within terms of maturity. I know girls can become pregnant at a very young age, but at her physical state of maturity that’s going to take a toll on her body that it wouldn’t for the more mature females, and we probably would have seen signs of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:37 pm Bagheera wrote: That's interesting to me. I'm also surprised you didn't seem to make the connection between Asuka as princess and Gendo as King. If Shinji is thus the prince that puts Mari's comments in a different light, and perhaps the slap as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:53 pm Na7e wrote: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14280/Princess-Asuka-and-the-Fairy-Tales/ There's a thread on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Fredsq » Sat Mar 07, 2015 8:50 am I’ve purely attributed Asuka’s grown up actions to her being 14 years older than Shinji and having gone through 14 (or slightly less) years of apocalypse. Not to her being married or having kids, that doesn’t seem Anno-esque. To me Wille has reached a phase where they’re more like a hive than a human colony: they do what’s best for the whole of mankind, so they’d have peasants that would reproduce, not their first tier fighters

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Mon Aug 17, 2015 3:16 pm There's one point in the OP that sticks out to me - the suggestion that Asuka has been in an abusive relationship, hence her idealisation of Shinji as some sort of "Prince Charming". If there's anyone most likely to be that abusive partner, why not Bardiel?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Aug 17, 2015 3:57 pm You have some interesting observations in there - although I'll join the rest of the crowd in pointing out the problem with the Toji/Asuka abusive relationship. The fact that Shinji gets an old shirt of Toji's in 3.0 suggests strongly that Toji is dead - either killed in the Zeruel battle or turned into a Failure of Infinity. Although it's perfectly possible to see Toji again later, there isn't so much of a mention of him, or Kensuke, or Hikari, by any character in the series, so even if they survived they are no longer relevant to the plot. Koji Takao mentions Kaji, which suggests that Kaji is still alive and at it.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Mon Aug 17, 2015 4:03 pm I thought we already established the the FoI were not transformed humans, but formed from Liliths LCL pool and "phased" through the Geofront surface (think GNR in EoE)
